response: The internet originated from 1960s US defense research, with ARPANET first demonstrating networking in 1969. Key milestones include the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983, Tim Berners-Lee's invention of the World Wide Web in 1989, and the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser, which popularized web access. After the dot-com boom and crash (late 1990s–2001), survivors like Amazon and Google became industry giants, while social media (mid-2000s) and the 2007 iPhone-driven mobile revolution further expanded connectivity. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins modern life across commerce, communication, and entertainment.
